g.text: OS Kernel: Preemptive Round-Robin Scheduling Logic

div.p: 1. Interrupt Entry: The Timer (PIT) triggers IRQ0. Hardware pushes SS:ESP, EFLAGS, CS:EIP onto the current kernel stack. The ASM handler saves remaining registers (pushad).
2. Scheduling Decision: scheduler_tick() determines the current process has exhausted its quantum. It moves Proc A from Running to Ready and selects Proc B.

ul.li: Save Phase: Current ESP is stored in pcb_a->k_esp.
Swap Phase: CPU ESP is updated to pcb_b->k_esp.
Address Space: CR3 is reloaded if address spaces differ, forcing a TLB flush.
Task State: TSS.ESP0 is updated so future interrupts land on Proc B's kernel stack.
Restore Phase: popad and iretd return execution to Proc B at its last known EIP.

g.title: Head of Queue -> Transition to Running

g.text: EAX, EBX, ECX, EDX, ESI, EDI
Select Next Task from Ready Queue

g.title: Head of Queue -> Transition to Running
